2605.03462.title: Learning Generalizable Action Representations via Pre-training AEMG
2605.03462.paper_summary: A fundamental role in decoding human motor intent and enabling intuitive human-computer interaction is played by electromyography (EMG). However, its generalization capability across subjects, devices, and tasks remains substantially limited by data heterogeneity, label scarcity, and the lack of a unified representational framework. To bridge this gap, we propose Any Electromyography (AEMG), the first large-scale, self-supervised representation learning framework for EMG. AEMG reconceptualizes neuromuscular dynamics linguistically, utilizing a novel Neuromuscular Contraction Tokenizer (NCT) to translate discrete muscle contractions into structural words and temporal activation patterns into coherent sentences. Furthermore, we compile the largest cross-device EMG signal vocabulary to date, enabling seamless transfer across arbitrary channel topologies and sampling rates. Experiments demonstrate that AEMG improves the zero-shot leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) accuracy by 5.79-9.25% compared to six state-of-the-art baselines, and achieves more than 90% few-shot adaptation performance with only 5% of target user data. Our work has proposed the concept of EMG signals as a cross-device physiological language, learned their grammar from massive amounts of data, and laid the groundwork for a single-training, universally applicable EMG foundation model.
2605.03462.paper_summary_zh: 一個基本的角色在於解碼人類運動意圖並促進直觀的人機互動，這是由肌電圖（EMG）所扮演的。然而，其在不同主體、設備和任務之間的泛化能力仍然受到數據異質性、標籤稀缺以及缺乏統一表示框架的實質限制。為了彌補這一差距，我們提出了任何肌電圖（AEMG），這是第一個大規模、自我監督的肌電圖表示學習框架。AEMG 從語言學的角度重新概念化神經肌肉動態，利用一種新穎的神經肌肉收縮標記器（NCT）將離散的肌肉收縮轉換為結構化的詞語，並將時間激活模式轉換為連貫的句子。此外，我們編纂了迄今為止最大的跨設備肌電信號詞彙，實現了在任意通道拓撲和取樣率之間的無縫轉移。實驗表明，與六個最先進的基準相比，AEMG 在零-shot 留一主體法（LOSO）準確性上提高了 5.79-9.25%，並在僅使用 5% 的目標用戶數據的情況下實現了超過 90% 的少量樣本適應性能。我們的工作提出了將肌電信號視為跨設備生理語言的概念，從大量數據中學習其語法，並為單次訓練、普遍適用的肌電圖基礎模型奠定了基礎。
2605.03462.author: Zhenghao Huang et.al.
2605.03462.authors: Zhenghao Huang, Huilin Yao, Kaikai Wang, Lin Shu
